summary: As AI coding capabilities rapidly improve, developers shouldn't feel threatened because writing code was never the hard part of building systems. The real challenges lie in architecture, workflows, product design, and understanding users—skills that become even more critical as AI handles more grunt work. Engineers should embrace AI as an acceleration tool while developing product thinking and good taste.
key_points: AI model improvements are accelerating weekly, making yesterday's capabilities obsolete
Current AI economics are unsustainable—expect price hikes and enshittification eventually
Code is just one component of systems building; architecture, workflows, and user needs matter more
Engineers should develop product thinking and systems thinking to stay relevant
AI should be viewed as an acceleration tool, not a zero-sum competitor
money_quotes: Writing code was seldom the hard part. Code is only a part of the many things we must consider when building systems.
Enjoy these subsidized coding superpowers while they last, because the economics will eventually tighten (enshittification, price hikes, etc.).
Don't treat coding as a zero-sum game, where the AI wins, and you lose. Instead, view it as an opportunity to accelerate the delivery of what matters to your users.
I've been telling devs and data engineers to learn product and systems thinking, as these will be critical skills as we increasingly work alongside AI.
key_insight: The rise of AI coding tools shifts engineering value from writing code to designing systems, understanding users, and developing good product taste.
